      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "0222082c",
        "outputId": "99a5d4f6-5e8f-4d08-aa56-0812e20f3658"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile validation_pipeline.py\n",
        "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n",
        "\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "validation_pipeline.py\n",
        "ASSET: A6 (Spectral Fidelity & Provenance Module)\n",
        "VERSION: 2.0 (Phase 3 Scientific Mandate)\n",
        "CLASSIFICATION: Final Implementation Blueprint / Governance Instrument\n",
        "GOAL: Serves as the immutable source of truth that cryptographically binds\n",
        "      experimental intent (parameters) to scientific fact (spectral fidelity)\n",
        "      and Aletheia cognitive coherence.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import json\n",
        "import hashlib\n",
        "import sys\n",
        "import os\n",
        "import argparse\n",
        "import h5py\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "import pandas as pd\n",
        "from datetime import datetime, timezone\n",
        "from typing import Dict, Any, List, Tuple, Optional # <--- FIX APPLIED: Added Optional\n",
        "import tempfile # Added for temporary file handling\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- V2.0 DEPENDENCIES ---\n",
        "# Import the core analysis engine (CEPP v1.0 / Quantule Profiler)\n",
        "# This file (quantulemapper.py) must be in the same directory.\n",
        "try:\n",
        "    import quantulemapper_real as cep_profiler\n",
        "except ImportError:\n",
        "    print(\"FATAL: Could not import 'quantulemapper.py'.\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "    print(\"This file is the core Quantule Profiler (CEPP v1.0).\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "    sys.exit(1)\n",
        "\n",
        "# Import Scipy for new Aletheia Metrics\n",
        "try:\n",
        "    from scipy.signal import coherence as scipy_coherence\n",
        "    from scipy.stats import entropy as scipy_entropy\n",
        "except ImportError:\n",
        "    print(\"FATAL: Missing 'scipy'. Please install: pip install scipy\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "    sys.exit(1)\n",
        "\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- MODULE CONSTANTS ---\n",
        "SCHEMA_VERSION = \"SFP-v2.0-ARCS\" # Upgraded schema version\n",
        "\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "# SECTION 1: PROVENANCE KERNEL (EVIDENTIAL INTEGRITY)\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def generate_canonical_hash(params_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Generates a canonical, deterministic SHA-256 hash from a parameter dict.\n",
        "    This function now explicitly filters out non-canonical metadata like 'run_uuid' and 'config_hash'\n",
        "    to ensure consistency across components.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Create a filtered dictionary for hashing, excluding non-canonical keys\n",
        "        filtered_params = {k: v for k, v in params_dict.items() if k not in [\"run_uuid\", \"config_hash\", \"param_hash_legacy\"]}\n",
        "\n",
        "        canonical_string = json.dumps(\n",
        "            filtered_params,\n",
        "            sort_keys=True,\n",
        "            separators=(\n",
        "                ',', ':'\n",
        "            )\n",
        "        )\n",
        "        string_bytes = canonical_string.encode('utf-8')\n",
        "        hash_object = hashlib.sha256(string_bytes)\n",
        "        config_hash = hash_object.hexdigest()\n",
        "        return config_hash\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"[ProvenanceKernel Error] Failed to generate hash: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        raise\n",
        "\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "# SECTION 2: FIDELITY KERNEL (SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION)\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def run_quantule_profiler(\n",
        "    rho_history_path: str,\n",
        "    temp_file_path: Optional[str] = None # Added for explicit temporary file handling\n",
        ") -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Orchestrates the core scientific analysis by calling the\n",
        "    Quantule Profiler (CEPP v1.0 / quantulemapper.py).\n",
        "\n",
        "    This function replaces the v1.0 mock logic. It loads the HDF5 artifact,\n",
        "    saves it as a temporary .npy file (as required by the profiler's API),\n",
        "    and runs the full analysis.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    if temp_file_path is None:\n",
        "        # Create a temporary .npy file for the profiler to consume\n",
        "        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=\".npy\", delete=False) as tmp:\n",
        "            temp_file_path = tmp.name\n",
        "        _cleanup_temp_file = True\n",
        "    else:\n",
        "        _cleanup_temp_file = False\n",
        "\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # 1. Load HDF5 data (as required by Orchestrator)\n",
        "        with h5py.File(rho_history_path, 'r') as f:\n",
        "            # Load the full 4D stack\n",
        "            rho_history = f['rho_history'][:]\n",
        "\n",
        "        if rho_history.ndim != 4:\n",
        "            raise ValueError(f\"Input HDF5 'rho_history' is not 4D (t,x,y,z). Shape: {rho_history.shape}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 2. Convert to .npy\n",
        "        np.save(temp_file_path, rho_history)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 3. Run the Quantule Profiler (CEPP v2.0)\n",
        "        print(f\"[FidelityKernel] Calling Quantule Profiler (CEPP v2.0) on {temp_file_path}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # --- NEW \"FAIL LOUD\" PATCH ---\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            # This is the call that was failing\n",
        "            profiler_results = cep_profiler.analyze_4d(temp_file_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Extract metrics. If a key is missing, this will\n",
        "            # now raise a KeyError, which is *good*.\n",
        "            log_prime_sse = float(profiler_results[\"total_sse\"])\n",
        "            validation_status = profiler_results.get(\"validation_status\", \"FAIL: UNKNOWN\")\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Get Sprint 2 Falsifiability Metrics\n",
        "            metrics_sse_null_a = float(profiler_results[\"sse_null_phase_scramble\"])\n",
        "            metrics_sse_null_b = float(profiler_results[\"sse_null_target_shuffle\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"CRITICAL: CEPP Profiler failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "            # Re-raise the exception to fail the validation step.\n",
        "            # This will stop the orchestrator and show us the error.\n",
        "            raise\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 4. Extract key results for the SFP artifact\n",
        "        spectral_fidelity = {\n",
        "            \"validation_status\": validation_status,\n",
        "            \"log_prime_sse\": log_prime_sse,\n",
        "            \"scaling_factor_S\": profiler_results.get(\"scaling_factor_S\", 0.0),\n",
        "            \"dominant_peak_k\": profiler_results.get(\"dominant_peak_k\", 0.0),\n",
        "            \"analysis_protocol\": \"CEPP v2.0\",\n",
        "            \"prime_log_targets\": cep_profiler.LOG_PRIME_TARGETS.tolist(), # PATCH 1 APPLIED HERE\n",
        "            \"sse_null_phase_scramble\": metrics_sse_null_a,\n",
        "            \"sse_null_target_shuffle\": metrics_sse_null_b,\n",
        "            # New diagnostic fields:\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_main\": profiler_results.get(\"n_peaks_found_main\", 0),\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_main\": profiler_results.get(\"failure_reason_main\", None),\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_null_a\": profiler_results.get(\"n_peaks_found_null_a\", 0),\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_null_a\": profiler_results.get(\"failure_reason_null_a\", None),\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_null_b\": profiler_results.get(\"n_peaks_found_null_b\", 0),\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_null_b\": profiler_results.get(\"failure_reason_null_b\", None)\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Return the full set of results for the Aletheia Metrics\n",
        "        return {\n",
        "            \"spectral_fidelity\": spectral_fidelity,\n",
        "            \"classification_results\": profiler_results.get(\"csv_files\", {}),\n",
        "            \"raw_rho_final_state\": rho_history[-1, :, :, :] # Pass final state\n",
        "        }\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"[FidelityKernel Error] Failed during Quantule Profiler execution or data loading: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        raise # Re-raise to ensure orchestrator catches the failure\n",
        "    finally:\n",
        "        # Clean up the temporary .npy file if it was created by this function\n",
        "        if _cleanup_temp_file and temp_file_path and os.path.exists(temp_file_path):\n",
        "            os.remove(temp_file_path)\n",
        "\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "# SECTION 3: ALETHEIA COHERENCE METRICS (PHASE 3)\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def calculate_pcs(rho_final_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    [Phase 3] Calculates the Phase Coherence Score (PCS).\n",
        "    Analogue: Superfluid order parameter.\n",
        "    Implementation: Magnitude-squared coherence function.\n",
        "\n",
        "    We sample two different, parallel 1D rays from the final state\n",
        "    and measure their coherence.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Ensure enough data points for coherence calculation\n",
        "        if rho_final_state.shape[0] < 3 or rho_final_state.shape[1] < 3 or rho_final_state.shape[2] < 3:\n",
        "            return 0.0 # Not enough data for meaningful rays\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Sample two 1D rays from the middle of the state\n",
        "        center_idx = rho_final_state.shape[0] // 2\n",
        "        ray_1 = rho_final_state[center_idx, center_idx, :]\n",
        "        ray_2 = rho_final_state[center_idx + 1, center_idx + 1, :] # Offset ray\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Ensure rays have enough points\n",
        "        if ray_1.size < 2 or ray_2.size < 2:\n",
        "            return 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Calculate coherence\n",
        "        f, Cxy = scipy_coherence(ray_1, ray_2)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # PCS is the mean coherence across all frequencies\n",
        "        pcs_score = np.mean(Cxy)\n",
        "\n",
        "        if np.isnan(pcs_score):\n",
        "            return 0.0\n",
        "        return float(pcs_score)\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"[AletheiaMetrics] WARNING: PCS calculation failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        return 0.0 # Failed coherence is 0\n",
        "\n",
        "def calculate_pli(rho_final_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    [Phase 3] Calculates the Principled Localization Index (PLI).\n",
        "    Analogue: Mott Insulator phase.\n",
        "    Implementation: Inverse Participation Ratio (IPR).\n",
        "\n",
        "    IPR = sum(psi^4) / (sum(psi^2))^2\n",
        "    A value of 1.0 is perfectly localized (Mott), 1/N is perfectly delocalized (Superfluid).\n",
        "    We use the density field `rho` as our `psi^2` equivalent.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # Normalize the density field (rho is already > 0)\n",
        "        sum_rho = np.sum(rho_final_state)\n",
        "        if sum_rho == 0:\n",
        "            return 0.0\n",
        "        rho_norm = rho_final_state / sum_rho\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Calculate IPR on the normalized density\n",
        "        # IPR = sum(p_i^2)\n",
        "        pli_score = np.sum(rho_norm**2)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Scale by N to get a value between (0, 1)\n",
        "        N_cells = rho_final_state.size\n",
        "        pli_score_normalized = float(pli_score * N_cells)\n",
        "\n",
        "        if np.isnan(pli_score_normalized):\n",
        "            return 0.0\n",
        "        return pli_score_normalized\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"[AletheiaMetrics] WARNING: PLI calculation failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        return 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "def calculate_ic(rho_final_state: np.ndarray) -> float:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    [Phase 3] Calculates the Informational Compressibility (IC).\n",
        "    Analogue: Thermodynamic compressibility.\n",
        "    Implementation: K_I = dS / dE (numerical estimation).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    try:\n",
        "        # 1. Proxy for System Energy (E):\n",
        "        # We use the L2 norm of the field (sum of squares) as a simple energy proxy.\n",
        "        proxy_E = np.sum(rho_final_state**2)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 2. Proxy for System Entropy (S):\n",
        "        # We treat the normalized field as a probability distribution\n",
        "        # and calculate its Shannon entropy.\n",
        "        rho_flat = rho_final_state.flatten()\n",
        "        sum_rho_flat = np.sum(rho_flat)\n",
        "        if sum_rho_flat == 0:\n",
        "            return 0.0 # Cannot calculate entropy for zero field\n",
        "        rho_prob = rho_flat / sum_rho_flat\n",
        "        # Add epsilon to avoid log(0)\n",
        "        proxy_S = scipy_entropy(rho_prob + 1e-9)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # 3. Calculate IC = dS / dE\n",
        "        # We perturb the system slightly to estimate the derivative\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Create a tiny perturbation (add 0.1% energy)\n",
        "        epsilon = 0.001\n",
        "        rho_perturbed = rho_final_state * (1.0 + epsilon)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Calculate new E and S\n",
        "        proxy_E_p = np.sum(rho_perturbed**2)\n",
        "\n",
        "        rho_p_flat = rho_perturbed.flatten()\n",
        "        sum_rho_p_flat = np.sum(rho_p_flat)\n",
        "        if sum_rho_p_flat == 0:\n",
        "            return 0.0\n",
        "        rho_p_prob = rho_p_flat / sum_rho_p_flat\n",
        "        proxy_S_p = scipy_entropy(rho_p_prob + 1e-9)\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Numerical derivative\n",
        "        dE = proxy_E_p - proxy_E\n",
        "        dS = proxy_S_p - proxy_S\n",
        "\n",
        "        if dE == 0 or np.isnan(dE) or np.isnan(dS):\n",
        "            return 0.0 # Incompressible or calculation failed\n",
        "\n",
        "        ic_score = float(dS / dE)\n",
        "\n",
        "        if np.isnan(ic_score):\n",
        "            return 0.0\n",
        "        return ic_score\n",
        "\n",
        "    except Exception as e:\n",
        "        print(f\"[AletheiaMetrics] WARNING: IC calculation failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        return 0.0\n",
        "\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "# SECTION 4: MAIN ORCHESTRATION (DRIVER HOOK)\n",
        "# ---\n",
        "\n",
        "def main():\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Main execution entry point for the SFP Module (v2.0).\n",
        "    Orchestrates the Quantule Profiler (CEPP), Provenance Kernel,\n",
        "    and Aletheia Metrics calculations.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n",
        "        description=\"Spectral Fidelity & Provenance (SFP) Module (Asset A6, v2.0)\"\n",
        "    )\n",
        "    parser.add_argument(\n",
        "        \"--input\",\n",
        "        type=str,\n",
        "        required=True,\n",
        "        help=\"Path to the input rho_history.h5 data artifact.\"\n",
        "    )\n",
        "    parser.add_argument(\n",
        "        \"--params\",\n",
        "        type=str,\n",
        "        required=True,\n",
        "        help=\"Path to the parameters.json file for this run.\"\n",
        "    )\n",
        "    parser.add_argument(\n",
        "        \"--output_dir\",\n",
        "        type=str,\n",
        "        default=\".\",\n",
        "        help=\"Directory to save the provenance.json and atlas CSVs.\"\n",
        "    )\n",
        "    args = parser.parse_args()\n",
        "\n",

      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"

        "id": "d3ce0617",
        "outputId": "7447361d-cdf4-4ad1-db0e-f5b8296aad0a"

      "source": [
        "%%writefile gravity/unified_omega.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "gravity/unified_omega.py (Sprint 1 - Patched)\n",
        "Single source of truth for the IRER Unified Gravity derivation.\n",
        "Implements the analytical solution for the conformal factor Omega(rho)\n",
        "and the emergent metric g_munu.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "from typing import Dict\n",
        "\n",
        "@jax.jit\n",
        "def jnp_derive_metric_from_rho(\n",
        "    rho: jnp.ndarray,\n",
        "    fmia_params: Dict,\n",
        "    epsilon: float = 1e-10\n",
        ") -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    [THEORETICAL BRIDGE] Derives the emergent spacetime metric g_munu directly\n",
        "    from the Resonance Density (rho) field.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Implements the analytical solution: g_munu = Omega^2 * eta_munu\n",
        "    Where Omega(rho) = (rho_vac / rho)^(a/2)\n",
        "    As derived in the Declaration of Intellectual Provenance (Section 5.3).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Get parameters from the derivation using the correct param_* keys\n",
        "    rho_vac = fmia_params.get('param_rho_vac', 1.0)\n",
        "    a_coupling = fmia_params.get('param_a_coupling', 1.0)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Add stabilization (mask rho <= 0)\n",
        "    rho_safe = jnp.maximum(rho, epsilon)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1. Calculate Omega^2 = (rho_vac / rho)^a\n",
        "    omega_squared = (rho_vac / rho_safe)**a_coupling\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clip the result to prevent NaN/Inf propagation\n",
        "    omega_squared = jnp.clip(omega_squared, 1e-12, 1e12)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. Construct the 4x4xNxNxN metric\n",
        "    grid_shape = rho.shape\n",
        "    g_munu = jnp.zeros((4, 4) + grid_shape)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # We assume eta_munu = diag(-1, 1, 1, 1)\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[0, 0, ...].set(-omega_squared) # g_00\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[1, 1, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_xx\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[2, 2, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_yy\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[3, 3, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_zz\n",
        "\n",
        "    return g_munu"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",
          "text": [
            "Writing gravity/unified_omega.py\n"
          ]
        }

        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "To implement the requested changes, I need to modify the `aste_hunter.py` file. This involves updating the `self.fieldnames` list, extracting the new diagnostic fields, and integrating a new fitness function that leverages falsifiability metrics into the `process_generation_results` method. I will use `%%writefile` to overwrite the existing file with the updated code.\n",

      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "dfd0acde",
        "outputId": "b0fc9be2-f1e1-4cc3-d3b4-f77ed6b05eb8"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile aste_hunter.py\n",
        "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n",
        "\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "aste_hunter.py\n",
        "CLASSIFICATION: Adaptive Learning Engine (ASTE V1.0)\n",
        "GOAL: Acts as the \"Brain\" of the ASTE. It reads validation reports\n",
        "      (provenance.json) from the SFP module, updates its internal\n",
        "      ledger, and applies an evolutionary algorithm to breed a\n",
        "      new generation of parameters that minimize the log_prime_sse.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import os\n",
        "import json\n",
        "import csv\n",
        "import random\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional\n",
        "import sys # Added for stderr\n",
        "import uuid # Added for temporary hash generation\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Configuration ---\n",
        "LEDGER_FILENAME = \"simulation_ledger.csv\"\n",
        "PROVENANCE_DIR = \"provenance_reports\" # Where the Validator saves reports\n",
        "SSE_METRIC_KEY = \"log_prime_sse\"\n",
        "HASH_KEY = \"config_hash\"\n",
        "\n",
        "# Evolutionary Algorithm Parameters\n",
        "TOURNAMENT_SIZE = 3\n",
        "MUTATION_RATE = 0.1\n",
        "MUTATION_STRENGTH = 0.05\n",
        "\n",
        "class Hunter:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the core evolutionary \"hunt\" logic.\n",
        "    Manages a population of parameters stored in a ledger\n",
        "    and breeds new generations to minimize SSE.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    def __init__(self, ledger_file: str = LEDGER_FILENAME):\n",
        "        self.ledger_file = ledger_file\n",
        "        self.fieldnames = [\n",
        "            HASH_KEY,\n",
        "            SSE_METRIC_KEY,\n",
        "            \"fitness\",\n",
        "            \"generation\",\n",
        "            \"param_D\",          # Metric-Aware Diffusion\n",
        "            \"param_eta\",        # Damping\n",
        "            \"param_rho_vac\",    # Derived metric param\n",
        "            \"param_a_coupling\",  # Derived metric param\n",
        "            \"sse_null_phase_scramble\", # SPRINT 2\n",
        "            \"sse_null_target_shuffle\",  # SPRINT 2\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_main\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_main\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_null_a\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_null_a\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_null_b\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_null_b\"  # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "        ]\n",
        "        self.population = self._load_ledger()\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Initialized. Loaded {len(self.population)} runs from {self.ledger_file}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _load_ledger(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Loads the historical population from the CSV ledger.\"\"\"\n",
        "        if not os.path.exists(self.ledger_file):\n",
        "            # Create an empty ledger if it doesn't exist\n",
        "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
        "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames)\n",
        "                writer.writeheader()\n",
        "            return []\n",
        "\n",
        "        population = []\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'r') as f:\n",
        "                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n",
        "                # Filter out fields from the CSV that are not in self.fieldnames (e.g. older schema)\n",
        "                # This ensures forward compatibility if fields are added.\n",
        "                current_fieldnames_set = set(self.fieldnames)\n",
        "                for row in reader:\n",
        "                    filtered_row = {k: v for k, v in row.items() if k in current_fieldnames_set}\n",
        "                    # Convert numerical strings back to floats/ints\n",
        "                    for key in self.fieldnames:\n",
        "                        if key in filtered_row and filtered_row[key] is not None and filtered_row[key] != '':\n",
        "                            try:\n",
        "                                if key == \"generation\":\n",
        "                                    filtered_row[key] = int(float(filtered_row[key])) # Ensure generation is an integer\n",
        "                                elif key.startswith('param_') or key.startswith('sse_') or key == SSE_METRIC_KEY or key == 'fitness':\n",
        "                                    filtered_row[key] = float(filtered_row[key])\n",
        "                                elif key.startswith('n_peaks_found'):\n",
        "                                    filtered_row[key] = int(float(filtered_row[key]))\n",
        "                            except ValueError:\n",
        "                                filtered_row[key] = None # Keep as None if conversion fails\n",
        "                        elif key not in filtered_row: # Ensure all expected fields are present, even if None\n",
        "                            filtered_row[key] = None\n",
        "\n",
        "                    population.append(filtered_row)\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to load ledger: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        return population\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _save_ledger(self):\n",
        "        \"\"\"Saves the entire population back to the CSV ledger.\"\"\"\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
        "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames, extrasaction='ignore')\n",
        "                writer.writeheader()\n",
        "                writer.writerows(self.population)\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to save ledger: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "\n",
        "    def process_generation_results(self, provenance_dir: str, job_hashes: List[str]):\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        MANDATE: Reads new provenance.json files, calculates fitness,\n",
        "        and updates the internal ledger.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Processing {len(job_hashes)} new results from {provenance_dir}...\")\n",
        "        new_runs_processed = 0\n",
        "        for config_hash in job_hashes:\n",
        "            report_path = os.path.join(provenance_dir, f\"provenance_{config_hash}.json\")\n",
        "\n",
        "            try:\n",
        "                with open(report_path, 'r') as f:\n",
        "                    data = json.load(f)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Extract metrics\n",
        "                sse = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"][SSE_METRIC_KEY]\n",
        "                sse_null_phase_scramble = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"sse_null_phase_scramble\", np.nan)\n",
        "                sse_null_target_shuffle = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"sse_null_target_shuffle\", np.nan)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Extract new diagnostic fields\n",
        "                n_peaks_found_main = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"n_peaks_found_main\", 0)\n",
        "                failure_reason_main = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"failure_reason_main\", None)\n",
        "                n_peaks_found_null_a = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"n_peaks_found_null_a\", 0)\n",
        "                failure_reason_null_a = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"failure_reason_null_a\", None)\n",
        "                n_peaks_found_null_b = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"n_peaks_found_null_b\", 0)\n",
        "                failure_reason_null_b = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"failure_reason_null_b\", None)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # --- New Falsifiability-Driven Fitness Calculation ---\n",
        "                base_fitness = 1.0 / (sse + 1e-9)\n",
        "                falsifiability_penalty = 1.0 # Start with no penalty\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Penalize if main run fails to find peaks, or other issues\n",
        "                if failure_reason_main is not None and sse >= 998.0: # 998.0/999.0 are failure codes\n",
        "                    falsifiability_penalty *= 0.1 # Significant penalty for outright failure\n",
        "\n",
        "                # If the main SSE is 'good' (low), but the nulls are not significantly higher\n",
        "                # This indicates a lack of falsifiability (i.e., the effect isn't real)\n",
        "                if sse < 1.0: # Check if main SSE is low enough to be considered 'good'\n",
        "                    # Null A check: if null A SSE is NOT significantly higher, apply penalty\n",
        "                    # (997.0 is specific code from profiler for Null A not differentiating)\n",
        "                    if sse_null_phase_scramble == 997.0:\n",
        "                         falsifiability_penalty *= 0.2 # Penalty for Null A not differentiating\n",
        "\n",
        "                    # Null B check: if null B SSE is NOT significantly higher, apply penalty\n",
        "                    # (996.0 is specific code from profiler for Null B not differentiating)\n",
        "                    if sse_null_target_shuffle == 996.0:\n",
        "                         falsifiability_penalty *= 0.2 # Penalty for Null B not differentiating\n",
        "\n",
        "                fitness = base_fitness * falsifiability_penalty\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Find the run in our population and update it\n",
        "                found = False\n",
        "                for run in self.population:\n",
        "                    if run[HASH_KEY] == config_hash:\n",
        "                        run[SSE_METRIC_KEY] = sse\n",
        "                        run[\"fitness\"] = fitness\n",
        "                        run[\"sse_null_phase_scramble\"] = sse_null_phase_scramble\n",
        "                        run[\"sse_null_target_shuffle\"] = sse_null_target_shuffle\n",
        "                        # Store new diagnostic fields\n",
        "                        run[\"n_peaks_found_main\"] = n_peaks_found_main\n",
        "                        run[\"failure_reason_main\"] = failure_reason_main\n",
        "                        run[\"n_peaks_found_null_a\"] = n_peaks_found_null_a\n",
        "                        run[\"failure_reason_null_a\"] = failure_reason_null_a\n",
        "                        run[\"n_peaks_found_null_b\"] = n_peaks_found_null_b\n",
        "                        run[\"failure_reason_null_b\"] = failure_reason_null_b\n",
        "                        found = True\n",
        "                        break\n",
        "\n",
        "                if found:\n",
        "                    new_runs_processed += 1\n",
        "                else:\n",
        "                    print(f\"[Hunter Warning] Hash {config_hash} found in JSON but not in population ledger.\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "\n",
        "            except FileNotFoundError:\n",
        "                print(f\"[Hunter Warning] Provenance file not found: {report_path}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "            except Exception as e:\n",
        "                print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to parse {report_path}: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Successfully processed and updated {new_runs_processed} runs.\")\n",
        "        self._save_ledger()\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _select_parent(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Selects one parent using tournament selection.\"\"\"\n",
        "        # Filter out runs with no fitness (i.e., failed runs that weren't processed)\n",
        "        eligible_population = [r for r in self.population if r.get(\"fitness\") is not None]\n",
        "        if not eligible_population:\n",
        "            raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot select parent: no runs with valid fitness in population.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Pick N random individuals from the eligible population\n",
        "        tournament = random.sample(eligible_population, min(TOURNAMENT_SIZE, len(eligible_population)))\n",
        "\n",
        "        # The winner is the one with the highest fitness\n",
        "        winner = max(tournament, key=lambda x: x.get(\"fitness\", 0.0))\n",
        "        return winner\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _crossover(self, parent1: Dict[str, Any], parent2: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Performs simple average crossover on parameters.\"\"\"\n",
        "        child_params = {\n",
        "            \"param_D\": (parent1[\"param_D\"] + parent2[\"param_D\"]) / 2.0,\n",
        "            \"param_eta\": (parent1[\"param_eta\"] + parent2[\"param_eta\"]) / 2.0,\n",
        "            \"param_rho_vac\": (parent1[\"param_rho_vac\"] + parent2[\"param_rho_vac\"]) / 2.0,\n",
        "            \"param_a_coupling\": (parent1[\"param_a_coupling\"] + parent2[\"param_a_coupling\"]) / 2.0\n",
        "        }\n",
        "        return child_params\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _mutate(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Applies random mutation to parameters.\"\"\"\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_D\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_D\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_D\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_eta\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_eta\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_eta\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_rho_vac\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_rho_vac\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_rho_vac\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_a_coupling\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_a_coupling\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_a_coupling\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        return params\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_next_generation(self, population_size: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Breeds a new generation of parameters.\n",
        "        This is the main function called by the Orchestrator.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        new_generation_params = []\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Filter out runs with no fitness for breeding\n",
        "        eligible_population = [r for r in self.population if r.get(\"fitness\") is not None]\n",
        "\n",
        "        if not eligible_population:\n",
        "            # Generation 0 or all previous runs failed: Create a random population\n",
        "            print(\"[Hunter] No eligible population found. Generating random Generation 0.\")\n",
        "            for _ in range(population_size):\n",
        "                params = {\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": np.random.uniform(0.1, 2.0),\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.5),\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": np.random.uniform(0.5, 1.5),\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": np.random.uniform(0.5, 1.5)\n",
        "                }\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append(params)\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            # Breed a new generation from the existing population\n",
        "            current_gen = self.get_current_generation()\n",
        "            print(f\"[Hunter] Breeding Generation {current_gen}...\")\n",
        "            # Sort by fitness (highest first)\n",
        "            sorted_population = sorted(eligible_population, key=lambda x: x.get(\"fitness\", 0.0), reverse=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Elitism: Keep the top 2 best individuals\n",
        "            # Ensure there are at least 2 individuals in sorted_population for elitism\n",
        "            if len(sorted_population) >= 2:\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append({\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": sorted_population[0][\"param_D\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": sorted_population[0][\"param_eta\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": sorted_population[0][\"param_rho_vac\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": sorted_population[0][\"param_a_coupling\"]\n",
        "                })\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append({\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": sorted_population[1][\"param_D\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": sorted_population[1][\"param_eta\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": sorted_population[1][\"param_rho_vac\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": sorted_population[1][\"param_a_coupling\"]\n",
        "                })\n",
        "            elif len(sorted_population) == 1: # If only one eligible, just add it once\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append({\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": sorted_population[0][\"param_D\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": sorted_population[0][\"param_eta\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": sorted_population[0][\"param_rho_vac\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": sorted_population[0][\"param_a_coupling\"]\n",
        "                })\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Breed the rest\n",
        "            for _ in range(population_size - len(new_generation_params)):\n",
        "                parent1 = self._select_parent()\n",
        "                parent2 = self._select_parent()\n",
        "                child_params = self._crossover(parent1, parent2)\n",
        "                mutated_child_params = self._mutate(child_params)\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append(mutated_child_params)\n",
        "\n",
        "        return new_generation_params # Return raw params to Orchestrator\n",
        "\n",
        "    def register_new_jobs(self, job_list: List[Dict[str, Any]]):\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Called by the Orchestrator *after* it has generated\n",
        "        canonical hashes for the new jobs.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        # Initialize new diagnostic fields for newly registered jobs to None\n",
        "        for job in job_list:\n",
        "            job[\"n_peaks_found_main\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"failure_reason_main\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"n_peaks_found_null_a\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"failure_reason_null_a\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"n_peaks_found_null_b\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"failure_reason_null_b\"] = None\n",
        "\n",
        "        self.population.extend(job_list)\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Registered {len(job_list)} new jobs in ledger.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_best_run(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Utility to get the best-performing run from the ledger.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        if not self.population:\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        valid_runs = [r for r in self.population if r.get(\"fitness\") is not None]\n",
        "        if not valid_runs:\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        return max(valid_runs, key=lambda x: x[\"fitness\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_current_generation(self) -> int:\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Determines the next generation number to breed.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        if not self.population:\n",
        "            return 0\n",
        "        # Filter out runs where 'generation' might be None or invalid\n",
        "        valid_generations = [run['generation'] for run in self.population if 'generation' in run and run['generation'] is not None]\n",
        "        if not valid_generations:\n",
        "            return 0\n",
        "        return max(valid_generations) + 1\n"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,
      "outputs": [
        {
          "output_type": "stream",
          "name": "stdout",

      "source": [
        "%%writefile gravity/unified_omega.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "gravity/unified_omega.py (Sprint 1 - Patched)\n",
        "Single source of truth for the IRER Unified Gravity derivation.\n",
        "Implements the analytical solution for the conformal factor Omega(rho)\n",
        "and the emergent metric g_munu.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "from typing import Dict\n",
        "\n",
        "@jax.jit\n",
        "def jnp_derive_metric_from_rho(\n",
        "    rho: jnp.ndarray,\n",
        "    fmia_params: Dict,\n",
        "    epsilon: float = 1e-10\n",
        ") -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    [THEORETICAL BRIDGE] Derives the emergent spacetime metric g_munu directly\n",
        "    from the Resonance Density (rho) field.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Implements the analytical solution: g_munu = Omega^2 * eta_munu\n",
        "    Where Omega(rho) = (rho_vac / rho)^(a/2)\n",
        "    As derived in the Declaration of Intellectual Provenance (Section 5.3).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Get parameters from the derivation using the correct param_* keys\n",
        "    rho_vac = fmia_params.get('param_rho_vac', 1.0)\n",
        "    a_coupling = fmia_params.get('param_a_coupling', 1.0)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Add stabilization (mask rho <= 0)\n",
        "    rho_safe = jnp.maximum(rho, epsilon)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1. Calculate Omega^2 = (rho_vac / rho)^a\n",
        "    omega_squared = (rho_vac / rho_safe)**a_coupling\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clip the result to prevent NaN/Inf propagation\n",
        "    omega_squared = jnp.clip(omega_squared, 1e-12, 1e12)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. Construct the 4x4xNxNxN metric\n",
        "    grid_shape = rho.shape\n",
        "    g_munu = jnp.zeros((4, 4) + grid_shape)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # We assume eta_munu = diag(-1, 1, 1, 1)\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[0, 0, ...].set(-omega_squared) # g_00\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[1, 1, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_xx\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[2, 2, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_yy\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[3, 3, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_zz\n",
        "\n",
        "    return g_munu"
      ],

            "  First file in 'provenance_reports': 5a8282aff727fa4d506f0b52399800c7b40e03d60d912aa49709f17c6d8a6209_quantule_events.csv\n"
          ]
        }

It generated new `simulation_data`, `input_configs`, and `provenance_reports` directories. The `simulation_ledger.csv` was updated with 8 new runs, now including diagnostic fields such as `n_peaks_found_main` and `failure_reason_main`, and the falsifiability-driven fitness values.\n",
        "2.  **What is the state of the new generation's results, including the best-performing run and confirmation that metrics are now being recorded properly?**\n",
        "    The `simulation_ledger.csv` contains data for 8 runs across two generations. The new diagnostic fields are now being recorded. However, most runs still show `log_prime_sse` values of `999.0` (indicating failure to find peaks), which results in a low `fitness` of `0.0001`. The best-performing run, identified by its highest fitness, is from Generation 0 (config_hash: `1e593149c66e89ed5385be951e233aecb8c1879e1c2690...`). It has a `log_prime_sse` of `999.0` and a `fitness` of `0.0001`. Its `failure_reason_main` indicates \"No peaks found in spectrum\", and similarly for null runs. This suggests that while all metrics are now correctly being *recorded*, the underlying simulations are still largely failing to produce meaningful spectral fidelity for these parameters, leading to uniform low fitness scores.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Data Analysis Key Findings\n",
        "*   The `aste_hunter.py` was successfully updated to include new diagnostic fields (`n_peaks_found_main`, `failure_reason_main`, `n_peaks_found_null_a`, `failure_reason_null_a`, `n_peaks_found_null_b`, `failure_reason_null_b`) in its `self.fieldnames` and to extract these from the `provenance.json` reports.\n",
        "*   A new falsifiability-driven fitness function was implemented in `aste_hunter.py`, which penalizes runs that fail to find peaks or where null hypotheses do not adequately differentiate from the main run. This updated fitness is now correctly reflected in `simulation_ledger.csv`.\n",
        "*   The orchestrator executed two full generations of simulations, correctly populating `simulation_data`, `input_configs`, and `provenance_reports` directories.\n",
        "*   The `simulation_ledger.csv` now clearly shows diagnostic reasons, with most runs indicating \"No peaks found in spectrum\" for `failure_reason_main`, which explains the uniformly high `log_prime_sse` values of `999.0` and correspondingly low `fitness` values of `0.0001` across most entries.\n",
        "*   The `sse_null_phase_scramble` and `sse_null_target_shuffle` metrics for failed runs also reflect `999.0`, consistent with the main run's failure to find peaks.\n",
        "\n",
        "### Insights or Next Steps\n",
        "*   While the data recording and fitness calculation mechanisms are now robust and correctly reflecting diagnostic information, the core issue of simulations failing to produce detectable spectral fidelity (as indicated by \"No peaks found in spectrum\") remains. This is the primary driver of the low fitness values.\n",
        "*   The next critical step is to investigate the parameters themselves or the simulation setup (`worker_unified.py`) to understand why spectral peaks are not being consistently generated or detected. This might involve adjusting the range of initial parameters, increasing simulation steps, or re-evaluating the physical model's ability to produce the desired spectral features within the current computational constraints."

      "source": [
        "## Refine Hunter Parameter Ranges\n",
        "\n",
        "### Subtask:\n",
        "Modify `aste_hunter.py` to adjust the initial parameter ranges for `param_D`, `param_eta`, `param_rho_vac`, and `param_a_coupling` when generating Generation 0, to encourage the exploration of a broader parameter space that might yield detectable spectral peaks.\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "markdown",
      "metadata": {
        "id": "36f2cf30"
      },
      "source": [
        "**Reasoning**:\n",
        "To implement the requested changes, I need to modify the `aste_hunter.py` file to adjust the initial parameter ranges for Generation 0. I will apply the new ranges as specified in the instructions and overwrite the existing file.\n",
        "\n"
      ]
    },
    {
      "cell_type": "code",
      "metadata": {
        "colab": {
          "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/"
        },
        "id": "99985de3",
        "outputId": "c35f61e4-b22c-4fdd-bb65-ff3c8b689c3e"
      },
      "source": [
        "%%writefile aste_hunter.py\n",
        "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n",
        "\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "aste_hunter.py\n",
        "CLASSIFICATION: Adaptive Learning Engine (ASTE V1.0)\n",
        "GOAL: Acts as the \"Brain\" of the ASTE. It reads validation reports\n",
        "      (provenance.json) from the SFP module, updates its internal\n",
        "      ledger, and applies an evolutionary algorithm to breed a\n",
        "      new generation of parameters that minimize the log_prime_sse.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import os\n",
        "import json\n",
        "import csv\n",
        "import random\n",
        "import numpy as np\n",
        "from typing import Dict, Any, List, Optional\n",
        "import sys # Added for stderr\n",
        "import uuid # Added for temporary hash generation\n",
        "\n",
        "# --- Configuration ---\n",
        "LEDGER_FILENAME = \"simulation_ledger.csv\"\n",
        "PROVENANCE_DIR = \"provenance_reports\" # Where the Validator saves reports\n",
        "SSE_METRIC_KEY = \"log_prime_sse\"\n",
        "HASH_KEY = \"config_hash\"\n",
        "\n",
        "# Evolutionary Algorithm Parameters\n",
        "TOURNAMENT_SIZE = 3\n",
        "MUTATION_RATE = 0.1\n",
        "MUTATION_STRENGTH = 0.05\n",
        "\n",
        "class Hunter:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    Implements the core evolutionary \"hunt\" logic.\n",
        "    Manages a population of parameters stored in a ledger\n",
        "    and breeds new generations to minimize SSE.\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    def __init__(self, ledger_file: str = LEDGER_FILENAME):\n",
        "        self.ledger_file = ledger_file\n",
        "        self.fieldnames = [\n",
        "            HASH_KEY,\n",
        "            SSE_METRIC_KEY,\n",
        "            \"fitness\",\n",
        "            \"generation\",\n",
        "            \"param_D\",          # Metric-Aware Diffusion\n",
        "            \"param_eta\",        # Damping\n",
        "            \"param_rho_vac\",    # Derived metric param\n",
        "            \"param_a_coupling\",  # Derived metric param\n",
        "            \"sse_null_phase_scramble\", # SPRINT 2\n",
        "            \"sse_null_target_shuffle\",  # SPRINT 2\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_main\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_main\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_null_a\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_null_a\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"n_peaks_found_null_b\", # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "            \"failure_reason_null_b\"  # New Diagnostic Field\n",
        "        ]\n",
        "        self.population = self._load_ledger()\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Initialized. Loaded {len(self.population)} runs from {self.ledger_file}\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _load_ledger(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Loads the historical population from the CSV ledger.\"\"\"\n",
        "        if not os.path.exists(self.ledger_file):\n",
        "            # Create an empty ledger if it doesn't exist\n",
        "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
        "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames)\n",
        "                writer.writeheader()\n",
        "            return []\n",
        "\n",
        "        population = []\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'r') as f:\n",
        "                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n",
        "                # Filter out fields from the CSV that are not in self.fieldnames (e.g. older schema)\n",
        "                # This ensures forward compatibility if fields are added.\n",
        "                current_fieldnames_set = set(self.fieldnames)\n",
        "                for row in reader:\n",
        "                    filtered_row = {k: v for k, v in row.items() if k in current_fieldnames_set}\n",
        "                    # Convert numerical strings back to floats/ints\n",
        "                    for key in self.fieldnames:\n",
        "                        if key in filtered_row and filtered_row[key] is not None and filtered_row[key] != '':\n",
        "                            try:\n",
        "                                if key == \"generation\":\n",
        "                                    filtered_row[key] = int(float(filtered_row[key])) # Ensure generation is an integer\n",
        "                                elif key.startswith('param_') or key.startswith('sse_') or key == SSE_METRIC_KEY or key == 'fitness':\n",
        "                                    filtered_row[key] = float(filtered_row[key])\n",
        "                                elif key.startswith('n_peaks_found'):\n",
        "                                    filtered_row[key] = int(float(filtered_row[key]))\n",
        "                            except ValueError:\n",
        "                                filtered_row[key] = None # Keep as None if conversion fails\n",
        "                        elif key not in filtered_row: # Ensure all expected fields are present, even if None\n",
        "                            filtered_row[key] = None\n",
        "\n",
        "                    population.append(filtered_row)\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to load ledger: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "        return population\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _save_ledger(self):\n",
        "        \"\"\"Saves the entire population back to the CSV ledger.\"\"\"\n",
        "        try:\n",
        "            with open(self.ledger_file, 'w', newline='') as f:\n",
        "                writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=self.fieldnames, extrasaction='ignore')\n",
        "                writer.writeheader()\n",
        "                writer.writerows(self.population)\n",
        "        except Exception as e:\n",
        "            print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to save ledger: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "\n",
        "    def process_generation_results(self, provenance_dir: str, job_hashes: List[str]):\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        MANDATE: Reads new provenance.json files, calculates fitness,\n",
        "        and updates the internal ledger.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Processing {len(job_hashes)} new results from {provenance_dir}...\")\n",
        "        new_runs_processed = 0\n",
        "        for config_hash in job_hashes:\n",
        "            report_path = os.path.join(provenance_dir, f\"provenance_{config_hash}.json\")\n",
        "\n",
        "            try:\n",
        "                with open(report_path, 'r') as f:\n",
        "                    data = json.load(f)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Extract metrics\n",
        "                sse = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"][SSE_METRIC_KEY]\n",
        "                sse_null_phase_scramble = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"sse_null_phase_scramble\", np.nan)\n",
        "                sse_null_target_shuffle = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"sse_null_target_shuffle\", np.nan)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Extract new diagnostic fields\n",
        "                n_peaks_found_main = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"n_peaks_found_main\", 0)\n",
        "                failure_reason_main = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"failure_reason_main\", None)\n",
        "                n_peaks_found_null_a = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"n_peaks_found_null_a\", 0)\n",
        "                failure_reason_null_a = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"failure_reason_null_a\", None)\n",
        "                n_peaks_found_null_b = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"n_peaks_found_null_b\", 0)\n",
        "                failure_reason_null_b = data[\"spectral_fidelity\"].get(\"failure_reason_null_b\", None)\n",
        "\n",
        "                # --- New Falsifiability-Driven Fitness Calculation ---\n",
        "                base_fitness = 1.0 / (sse + 1e-9)\n",
        "                falsifiability_penalty = 1.0 # Start with no penalty\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Penalize if main run fails to find peaks, or other issues\n",
        "                if failure_reason_main is not None and sse >= 998.0: # 998.0/999.0 are failure codes\n",
        "                    falsifiability_penalty *= 0.1 # Significant penalty for outright failure\n",
        "\n",
        "                # If the main SSE is 'good' (low), but the nulls are not significantly higher\n",
        "                # This indicates a lack of falsifiability (i.e., the effect isn't real)\n",
        "                if sse < 1.0: # Check if main SSE is low enough to be considered 'good'\n",
        "                    # Null A check: if null A SSE is NOT significantly higher, apply penalty\n",
        "                    # (997.0 is specific code from profiler for Null A not differentiating)\n",
        "                    if sse_null_phase_scramble == 997.0:\n",
        "                         falsifiability_penalty *= 0.2 # Penalty for Null A not differentiating\n",
        "\n",
        "                    # Null B check: if null B SSE is NOT significantly higher, apply penalty\n",
        "                    # (996.0 is specific code from profiler for Null B not differentiating)\n",
        "                    if sse_null_target_shuffle == 996.0:\n",
        "                         falsifiability_penalty *= 0.2 # Penalty for Null B not differentiating\n",
        "\n",
        "                fitness = base_fitness * falsifiability_penalty\n",
        "\n",
        "                # Find the run in our population and update it\n",
        "                found = False\n",
        "                for run in self.population:\n",
        "                    if run[HASH_KEY] == config_hash:\n",
        "                        run[SSE_METRIC_KEY] = sse\n",
        "                        run[\"fitness\"] = fitness\n",
        "                        run[\"sse_null_phase_scramble\"] = sse_null_phase_scramble\n",
        "                        run[\"sse_null_target_shuffle\"] = sse_null_target_shuffle\n",
        "                        # Store new diagnostic fields\n",
        "                        run[\"n_peaks_found_main\"] = n_peaks_found_main\n",
        "                        run[\"failure_reason_main\"] = failure_reason_main\n",
        "                        run[\"n_peaks_found_null_a\"] = n_peaks_found_null_a\n",
        "                        run[\"failure_reason_null_a\"] = failure_reason_null_a\n",
        "                        run[\"n_peaks_found_null_b\"] = n_peaks_found_null_b\n",
        "                        run[\"failure_reason_null_b\"] = failure_reason_null_b\n",
        "                        found = True\n",
        "                        break\n",
        "\n",
        "                if found:\n",
        "                    new_runs_processed += 1\n",
        "                else:\n",
        "                    print(f\"[Hunter Warning] Hash {config_hash} found in JSON but not in population ledger.\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "\n",
        "            except FileNotFoundError:\n",
        "                print(f\"[Hunter Warning] Provenance file not found: {report_path}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "            except Exception as e:\n",
        "                print(f\"[Hunter Error] Failed to parse {report_path}: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n",
        "\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Successfully processed and updated {new_runs_processed} runs.\")\n",
        "        self._save_ledger()\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _select_parent(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Selects one parent using tournament selection.\"\"\"\n",
        "        # Filter out runs with no fitness (i.e., failed runs that weren't processed)\n",
        "        eligible_population = [r for r in self.population if r.get(\"fitness\") is not None]\n",
        "        if not eligible_population:\n",
        "            raise RuntimeError(\"Cannot select parent: no runs with valid fitness in population.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Pick N random individuals from the eligible population\n",
        "        tournament = random.sample(eligible_population, min(TOURNAMENT_SIZE, len(eligible_population)))\n",
        "\n",
        "        # The winner is the one with the highest fitness\n",
        "        winner = max(tournament, key=lambda x: x.get(\"fitness\", 0.0))\n",
        "        return winner\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _crossover(self, parent1: Dict[str, Any], parent2: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Performs simple average crossover on parameters.\"\"\"\n",
        "        child_params = {\n",
        "            \"param_D\": (parent1[\"param_D\"] + parent2[\"param_D\"]) / 2.0,\n",
        "            \"param_eta\": (parent1[\"param_eta\"] + parent2[\"param_eta\"]) / 2.0,\n",
        "            \"param_rho_vac\": (parent1[\"param_rho_vac\"] + parent2[\"param_rho_vac\"]) / 2.0,\n",
        "            \"param_a_coupling\": (parent1[\"param_a_coupling\"] + parent2[\"param_a_coupling\"]) / 2.0\n",
        "        }\n",
        "        return child_params\n",
        "\n",
        "    def _mutate(self, params: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"Applies random mutation to parameters.\"\"\"\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_D\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_D\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_D\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_eta\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_eta\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_eta\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_rho_vac\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_rho_vac\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_rho_vac\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        if random.random() < MUTATION_RATE:\n",
        "            params[\"param_a_coupling\"] += np.random.normal(0, MUTATION_STRENGTH)\n",
        "            params[\"param_a_coupling\"] = max(0.001, params[\"param_a_coupling\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "        return params\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_next_generation(self, population_size: int) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Breeds a new generation of parameters.\n",
        "        This is the main function called by the Orchestrator.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        new_generation_params = []\n",
        "\n",
        "        # Filter out runs with no fitness for breeding\n",
        "        eligible_population = [r for r in self.population if r.get(\"fitness\") is not None]\n",
        "\n",
        "        if not eligible_population:\n",
        "            # Generation 0 or all previous runs failed: Create a random population\n",
        "            print(\"[Hunter] No eligible population found. Generating random Generation 0.\")\n",
        "            for _ in range(population_size):\n",
        "                params = {\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": np.random.uniform(0.01, 5.0), # Adjusted range\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": np.random.uniform(0.001, 1.0), # Adjusted range\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": np.random.uniform(0.1, 2.0), # Adjusted range\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": np.random.uniform(0.1, 3.0) # Adjusted range\n",
        "                }\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append(params)\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            # Breed a new generation from the existing population\n",
        "            current_gen = self.get_current_generation()\n",
        "            print(f\"[Hunter] Breeding Generation {current_gen}...\")\n",
        "            # Sort by fitness (highest first)\n",
        "            sorted_population = sorted(eligible_population, key=lambda x: x.get(\"fitness\", 0.0), reverse=True)\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Elitism: Keep the top 2 best individuals\n",
        "            # Ensure there are at least 2 individuals in sorted_population for elitism\n",
        "            if len(sorted_population) >= 2:\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append({\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": sorted_population[0][\"param_D\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": sorted_population[0][\"param_eta\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": sorted_population[0][\"param_rho_vac\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": sorted_population[0][\"param_a_coupling\"]\n",
        "                })\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append({\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": sorted_population[1][\"param_D\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": sorted_population[1][\"param_eta\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": sorted_population[1][\"param_rho_vac\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": sorted_population[1][\"param_a_coupling\"]\n",
        "                })\n",
        "            elif len(sorted_population) == 1: # If only one eligible, just add it once\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append({\n",
        "                    \"param_D\": sorted_population[0][\"param_D\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_eta\": sorted_population[0][\"param_eta\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_rho_vac\": sorted_population[0][\"param_rho_vac\"],\n",
        "                    \"param_a_coupling\": sorted_population[0][\"param_a_coupling\"]\n",
        "                })\n",
        "\n",
        "            # Breed the rest\n",
        "            for _ in range(population_size - len(new_generation_params)):\n",
        "                parent1 = self._select_parent()\n",
        "                parent2 = self._select_parent()\n",
        "                child_params = self._crossover(parent1, parent2)\n",
        "                mutated_child_params = self._mutate(child_params)\n",
        "                new_generation_params.append(mutated_child_params)\n",
        "\n",
        "        return new_generation_params # Return raw params to Orchestrator\n",
        "\n",
        "    def register_new_jobs(self, job_list: List[Dict[str, Any]]):\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Called by the Orchestrator *after* it has generated\n",
        "        canonical hashes for the new jobs.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        # Initialize new diagnostic fields for newly registered jobs to None\n",
        "        for job in job_list:\n",
        "            job[\"n_peaks_found_main\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"failure_reason_main\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"n_peaks_found_null_a\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"failure_reason_null_a\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"n_peaks_found_null_b\"] = None\n",
        "            job[\"failure_reason_null_b\"] = None\n",
        "\n",
        "        self.population.extend(job_list)\n",
        "        print(f\"[Hunter] Registered {len(job_list)} new jobs in ledger.\")\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_best_run(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Utility to get the best-performing run from the ledger.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        if not self.population:\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        valid_runs = [r for r in self.population if r.get(\"fitness\") is not None]\n",
        "        if not valid_runs:\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        return max(valid_runs, key=lambda x: x[\"fitness\"])\n",
        "\n",
        "    def get_current_generation(self) -> int:\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Determines the next generation number to breed.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        if not self.population:\n",
        "            return 0\n",
        "        # Filter out runs where 'generation' might be None or invalid\n",
        "        valid_generations = [run['generation'] for run in self.population if 'generation' in run and run['generation'] is not None]\n",
        "        if not valid_generations:\n",
        "            return 0\n",
        "        return max(valid_generations) + 1\n"
      ],
      "execution_count": null,

        "%%writefile gravity/unified_omega.py\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "gravity/unified_omega.py (Sprint 1 - Patched)\n",
        "Single source of truth for the IRER Unified Gravity derivation.\n",
        "Implements the analytical solution for the conformal factor Omega(rho)\n",
        "and the emergent metric g_munu.\n",
        "\"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "import jax\n",
        "import jax.numpy as jnp\n",
        "from typing import Dict\n",
        "\n",
        "@jax.jit\n",
        "def jnp_derive_metric_from_rho(\n",
        "    rho: jnp.ndarray,\n",
        "    fmia_params: Dict,\n",
        "    epsilon: float = 1e-10\n",
        ") -> jnp.ndarray:\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "    [THEORETICAL BRIDGE] Derives the emergent spacetime metric g_munu directly\n",
        "    from the Resonance Density (rho) field.\n",
        "\n",
        "    Implements the analytical solution: g_munu = Omega^2 * eta_munu\n",
        "    Where Omega(rho) = (rho_vac / rho)^(a/2)\n",
        "    As derived in the Declaration of Intellectual Provenance (Section 5.3).\n",
        "    \"\"\"\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Get parameters from the derivation using the correct param_* keys\n",
        "    rho_vac = fmia_params.get('param_rho_vac', 1.0)\n",
        "    a_coupling = fmia_params.get('param_a_coupling', 1.0)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Add stabilization (mask rho <= 0)\n",
        "    rho_safe = jnp.maximum(rho, epsilon)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 1. Calculate Omega^2 = (rho_vac / rho)^a\n",
        "    omega_squared = (rho_vac / rho_safe)**a_coupling\n",
        "\n",
        "    # Clip the result to prevent NaN/Inf propagation\n",
        "    omega_squared = jnp.clip(omega_squared, 1e-12, 1e12)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # 2. Construct the 4x4xNxNxN metric\n",
        "    grid_shape = rho.shape\n",
        "    g_munu = jnp.zeros((4, 4) + grid_shape)\n",
        "\n",
        "    # We assume eta_munu = diag(-1, 1, 1, 1)\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[0, 0, ...].set(-omega_squared) # g_00\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[1, 1, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_xx\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[2, 2, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_yy\n",
        "    g_munu = g_munu.at[3, 3, ...].set(omega_squared)  # g_zz\n",
        "\n",
        "    return g_munu"
      ],
